content:
[S1]: After years of searching, I finally found the secret to living forever!  
[READER]: Wow, living forever sounds like a superpower! I wonder what it feels like to never grow old. I want to know what happens next.  

[S2]: Just as I was celebrating, a shadowy figure appeared—it was Death, but not scary at all.  
[READER]: Death isn’t scary? That’s surprising! I’m curious what Death wants from the person who can live forever.  

[S3]: Death smiled and handed me a shiny business card with a mysterious phone number on it.  
[READER]: A business card? That’s funny! I want to see what’s written on it and why Death gave it to them.  

[S4]: “When you realize living forever is not as fun as it sounds,” Death said, “call this number—I have a job offer for you.”  
[READER]: Living forever might not be fun? That’s unexpected! I want to know why it might be boring or hard.  

[S5]: Suddenly, I imagined all the birthdays, school days, and adventures that would never end or change.  
[READER]: Hmm, that sounds a bit lonely or tiring. I’m curious if the person will call Death or keep living forever.  

[S6]: I thought about calling the number, but what kind of job could Death offer to someone who never dies?  
[READER]: That’s a good question! I hope the story tells me more about the job and what happens if they call.  

[S7]: Just then, Death winked and vanished, leaving me holding the card and wondering about forever.  
[READER]: That’s a cool ending! I like it when stories leave a little mystery. Maybe the person will decide later.  

===BEGIN===  
After years of searching, I finally found the secret to living forever!  
Just as I was celebrating, a shadowy figure appeared—it was Death, but not scary at all.  
Death smiled and handed me a shiny business card with a mysterious phone number on it.  
“When you realize living forever is not as fun as it sounds,” Death said, “call this number—I have a job offer for you.”  
Suddenly, I imagined all the birthdays, school days, and adventures that would never end or change.  
I thought about calling the number, but what kind of job could Death offer to someone who never dies?  
Just then, Death winked and vanished, leaving me holding the card and wondering about forever.  
===END===
